hero.role: AI Ethics & Techno-Ethics, HCI, Information Studies, Requirements Engineering & Socio-technical Systems
hero.intro: Lecturer in Techno-Anthropology at Aalborg University and Assistant Lecturer at the IT University of Copenhagen. My roles span research, teaching, technology projects, and interdisciplinary collaboration.

now.title: I may be looking for my next boss—and my next office-mate.
now.body: Aalborg University is hiring an Assistant Professor in Responsible Technology Futures to join the Re-TECH research environment. We take the work seriously, but not ourselves all the time: the room is bright, the conversations are lively, and ideas have a habit of becoming papers, projects, teaching, and the occasional good laugh. If participatory design, engineering or interdisciplinary education sounds like your territory, perhaps the beautiful Copenhagen office is waiting for you.

reflection.title: Between two mornings
paragraphs: Some nights I am still awake when morning begins. As an immigrant living between places and time zones, I carry more than one clock inside me. Once, my days spilled easily into rooms full of friends; now I can be a night crawler bargaining with an early class—wondering whether my partner will wake soon and leave for work, whether tomorrow’s slides are ready, whether a deadline has quietly moved closer. And then: oh no, LinkedIn. Perhaps I should look.

Behind these smaller alarms lives a larger one. My parents are growing older far away. I call and hear a tired voice, a cough, or the quiet suggestion that something is missing. Distance turns love into questions I cannot always answer: Are they well? Did I call enough? What does it mean to build a life here while part of my heart keeps listening seven thousand kilometres away?

Still, life refuses to be only worry. The sun pours gold across the sky—goldy-gold, almost unreal. A ridiculous meme makes me laugh. Friends appear together on a phone screen, smiling from the other side of the world, and for a while the kilometres disappear. Then an email arrives: the paper is accepted. A colleague says something kind. A small accomplishment knocks at the door, and I catch myself smiling before anyone else can see.

Yes, life goes on. Time passes through unfinished slides, missed sleep, distant voices, good news, private laughter, and skies that keep changing colour. Night gives back the morning. The new day comes. And somehow, so do I.

group.title: AAU Research Group · Re-TECH
paragraphs: Re-TECH—Responsible Technology Futures—is an interdisciplinary research group at Aalborg University’s Department of Sustainability and Planning. The group studies how emerging and established technologies are shaped by institutions, policies, organisations, communities, professional practices, and everyday life.

Its research addresses the ethical, social, and ecological dimensions of technological change. Areas of interest include responsible AI, AI in public services, digital welfare, data governance, citizen science, participatory design and implementation, technology assessment, and the consequences of digital infrastructures for people and society.

Responsibility is approached as more than a technical property. Re-TECH examines how values, accountability, inclusion, sustainability, and public legitimacy are interpreted and negotiated across the design, implementation, governance, and use of technological systems.

My affiliation connects this environment to my teaching in Techno-Anthropology and to developing work on responsible AI, public digitalisation, participatory system design, requirements, and Erasmus+ EGALITARIAN.
compact_paragraphs: Re-TECH—Responsible Technology Futures—is an interdisciplinary research group at Aalborg University’s Department of Sustainability and Planning. It studies how technologies are shaped by institutions, organisations, communities, professional practice, and everyday life.

The group works across responsible AI, public digitalisation, digital welfare, data governance, citizen science, participatory design, technology assessment, and the social and ecological consequences of digital infrastructures.
My affiliation connects the group to my teaching and developing work on responsible AI, requirements, public digitalisation, participatory system design, and Erasmus+ EGALITARIAN.

topics.title: AI Ethics & Techno-Ethics
topics.body: Human values, responsibility, oversight, design justice, data and design bias, empathetic AI, marginalised communities, AI values, and Responsible Research & Innovation.

topics.title: Human-Computer Interaction
topics.body: Human-in-the-loop systems, usability, trust, explanation, evaluation, participatory design, personas, and how people adapt or challenge generative and agentic systems.

topics.title: Requirements Engineering
topics.body: Requirements ethnography, empirical software engineering, elicitation, workflows, developer experience, ethical assessment, ESG/CSRD reporting, and ISO compliance.

topics.title: Responsible AI & Governance
topics.body: Responsibility, accountability, value alignment, organisational governance, public legitimacy, standards, compliance, and the practical oversight of AI-enabled systems.

topics.title: Participatory Design & VSD
topics.body: Participatory inquiry, Value Sensitive Design, co-design, stakeholder values, personas, scenarios, workshops, and design justice.

topics.title: Socio-technical Systems
topics.body: Relations among technologies, people, organisations, institutions, infrastructures, information practices, and everyday work.

topics.title: AI for Software Engineering
topics.body: Foundation models, AI agents, developer tools, software work, empirical evaluation, requirements, and organisational adoption.

topics.title: Public-sector Digitalisation
topics.body: Public-sector system governance, organisational change, information practices, inclusion, institutional responsibility, algorithmic marginalisation, and accountability.

topics.title: Data Governance
topics.body: Data rights, infrastructures, standards, access, accountability, citizen science, bias, and responsible information practices.

outputs.title: Designing for Justice
outputs.body: The participatory Value Sensitive Design of a software system for cooperative waste management in Brazil.
outputs.compact_body: Accepted Science and Engineering Ethics paper on participatory Value Sensitive Design of a cooperative waste-management software system in Brazil.

outputs.title: Participatory Persona to AI Informant
outputs.authors: L. R. Rabbi & J. M. Pedersen
outputs.body: Two studies develop and evaluate persona-grounded generative-AI informants using Brazilian waste-picker workshop data.
outputs.compact_body: Two PAEE/ALE studies develop and evaluate persona-grounded generative-AI informants with Brazilian waste-pickers’ workshop data.

outputs.title: Does Persona-grounded AI Informant Hold Up?
outputs.authors: L. R. Rabbi & J. M. Pedersen
outputs.body: A comparative study evaluating a persona-grounded AI informant with Brazilian waste-picker workshop data.
outputs.compact_body: A comparative evaluation of a persona-grounded AI informant using waste-picker workshop data.

outputs.title: Value Alignment Analyser
outputs.body: A method, prototype, and manuscript examining how stakeholder values are translated into system requirements, design elements, and evaluation criteria.
outputs.compact_body: A method and prototype tracing stakeholder values into requirements, design elements, and evaluation criteria.

outputs.title: AI Paradigm Shift
outputs.body: A developing conceptual paper on the shift from model-centred machine learning toward foundation-model ecosystems, agentic workflows, and changing software practices.
outputs.compact_body: A conceptual paper on foundation-model ecosystems, agentic workflows, and changing software practices.

outputs.title: Data-driven vs Data-intensive Environmental-Marine Citizen Science
outputs.authors: A. Cenci, L. R. Rabbi & T. Pyhälahti
outputs.body: A comparative study of data-driven and data-intensive environmental-marine citizen science, participation, governance, and information infrastructure.
outputs.compact_body: Submitted study comparing data-driven and data-intensive environmental-marine citizen science, participation, and infrastructure.

outputs.title: Digital Mobilizer App
outputs.authors: J. M. da Silva et al., including L. R. Rabbi
outputs.body: Environmental education through a digital mobilizer and personas created with waste pickers.
outputs.compact_body: Published PAEE/ALE work on environmental education through a digital mobilizer and personas created with waste pickers.

outputs.title: AI Clones of Deceased Persons
outputs.body: A policy and ethics study of posthumous digital identity, data rights, personhood, consent, governance, and socio-cultural consequences.
outputs.compact_body: A policy and ethics study of posthumous digital identity, consent, personhood, data rights, and governance.

outputs.title: Value Sensitive Design of a Data Management System
outputs.body: A Data Management System for cooperative waste management in Brazil, developed through ethnography, value elicitation, participatory design, and requirements work.
outputs.compact_body: MSc project designing a data-management system for cooperative waste management through ethnography, value elicitation, participatory design, and requirements work.

outputs.title: Enhancing Transparency in Waste Management
outputs.authors: C. Bro, I. R. da Trindade, L. R. Rabbi & F. S. Arnfred
outputs.body: A peer-reviewed Techno-Anthropology project using participatory methods and prototyping to improve transparency in cooperative waste management.
outputs.compact_body: Peer-reviewed Techno-Anthropology project using participatory methods and prototyping to improve transparency in cooperative waste management.

outputs.title: Digital Location Tracking within Families
outputs.authors: S. B. Toft et al., including L. R. Rabbi
outputs.body: A peer-reviewed study of privacy, trust, independence, and ethical conflicts in digital location tracking within families.
outputs.compact_body: Peer-reviewed study of privacy, trust, independence, and ethical conflicts in digital location tracking within families.
